title: How To Pour Bottles for Patients
description: Ensure methadone take-home bottles are prepared, labeled, and sealed accurately and safely in accordance with clinic and regulatory standards.

steps.title: Open Methasoft
steps.directionHtml: Launch the Methasoft application on your workstation.

steps.title: Select the Correct Date
steps.directionHtml:
Choose the day you would like to pour bottles for. Ensure both Start Date and End Date are the same.

Example: To pour bottles for 11/04/2025, both dates should be 11/04/2025.

steps.title: Select Bottles to Pour
steps.directionHtml: Click on the bottles assigned for that date.

steps.title: Verify Dose and Volume
steps.directionHtml: Confirm the correct dose (in mg) and volume (in mL) for each bottle.

steps.title: Pour Methadone
steps.directionHtml: Using a calibrated measuring device, pour the exact amount of methadone into the bottle.

steps.title: Add Cherry Flavoring
steps.directionHtml: Add flavoring if required per clinic protocol.

steps.title: Seal the Bottle
steps.directionHtml: Securely cap each bottle to prevent leakage.

steps.title: Apply the Label
steps.directionHtml: Attach the pre-printed label to the bottle. Ensure it matches the patient name, dose, and date.

steps.title: Verify Label Information
steps.directionHtml: Double-check that all label details are correct.

steps.title: Document in Methasoft
steps.directionHtml: Mark the bottle as poured in the system.

steps.title: Store Properly
steps.directionHtml: Place sealed, labeled bottles in the designated storage area.

steps.title: Clean Work Area
steps.directionHtml: Wipe down the pouring station and dispose of any waste properly.
